mechanism: ACE inhibitors reduce aldosterone-mediated potassium excretion; potassium supplementation adds exogenous potassium load.
recommendation: Avoid routine potassium supplementation unless there is a clear indication and potassium/renal function are monitored.
monitoring: ECG if symptomatic or severe hyperkalemia suspected
source: Local Interaction Rule: ACEI + potassium v1

risk: Acute kidney injury, reduced antihypertensive effect, possible hyperkalemia
mechanism: NSAIDs constrict the afferent renal arteriole while ACE inhibitors dilate the efferent arteriole, reducing glomerular filtration pressure in susceptible patients.
recommendation: Use the shortest NSAID duration if unavoidable, avoid in high-risk renal/dehydrated patients, and check renal function and potassium when clinically relevant.
source: Local Interaction Rule: ACEI + NSAID v1

mechanism: ACE inhibitor/ARB + diuretic + NSAID can reduce renal perfusion and filtration through combined hemodynamic effects.
recommendation: Avoid the combination when possible. If unavoidable, ensure hydration, baseline renal function/electrolytes, short NSAID duration, and follow-up labs.
source: Local Clinical Rule: Triple whammy v1

mechanism: Amiodarone inhibits warfarin metabolism, increasing anticoagulant effect.
recommendation: Requires close INR monitoring and prescriber-led warfarin dose adjustment. Counsel for bleeding symptoms.
source: Local Interaction Rule: warfarin + amiodarone v1

mechanism: Alpha-1 blockade and PDE5 inhibition both lower vascular tone and can have additive blood-pressure lowering effects.
recommendation: Avoid unsafe self-combination. If prescribed together, use cautious timing/dosing and counsel about dizziness, syncope, and standing slowly.
source: Local Interaction Rule: alpha-blocker + PDE5 inhibitor v1

mechanism: ARBs reduce aldosterone-mediated potassium excretion; potassium supplements increase potassium load.
recommendation: Avoid routine potassium supplementation unless indicated and monitored.
source: Local Interaction Rule: ARB + potassium v1

risk: Bleeding risk due to combined anticoagulant and antiplatelet effect
mechanism: Warfarin anticoagulation plus aspirin platelet inhibition increases bleeding risk.
recommendation: Only combine when there is a clear prescriber-approved indication. Counsel for bleeding and monitor closely.
source: Local Interaction Rule: warfarin + aspirin v1
